target.title: docs(providers): merge GLM docs into Z.AI page
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Z.AI and GLM are actually the same provider in OpenClaw (`zai` provider, `ZAI_API_KEY`, Z.AI Chat Completions / Bearer auth), but the docs were split across `/providers/zai` (provider page) and `/providers/glm` (model-family page). The two pages duplicated setup guidance, drifted apart over time, and users entering from either side had to bounce between them to get the full picture.
- **Solution:** Merge the GLM page into `docs/providers/zai.md`, delete `docs/providers/glm.md`, and point every internal link / nav entry / i18n nav / redirect at `/providers/zai`. While merging, add a Property table and a Config example to the Z.AI page, and simplify Getting started from 3 steps to 2 (onboarding already sets the default model, so the "Set a default model" step is no longer needed).
- **What changed:**
  - `docs/providers/zai.md`: add Property table; add Config example section; correct the auto-detect wording (the `zai-api-key` choice **probes** supported endpoints with the API key — it does not infer from a key prefix); simplify Getting started from 3 to 2 steps; align Related cards with Model selection / Configuration reference.
  - Delete `docs/providers/glm.md` (content merged into `zai.md`). The legacy env var `Z_AI_API_KEY` (which the runtime still accepts via `src/infra/env.ts` and lists in `extensions/zai/openclaw.plugin.json`) is preserved in the consolidated docs — see the auth row of the Property table and the Auth details accordion.
  - `docs/docs.json`: change `/glm` destination from `/providers/glm` to `/providers/zai`; add new `/providers/glm` → `/providers/zai` redirect; remove `providers/glm` from navigation.
  - `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`: keep main's `<CardGroup>` structure; rename the GLM card to `Z.AI (GLM)` and update href to `/providers/zai`.
  - `docs/providers/index.md`, `docs/providers/models.md`, `docs/concepts/oauth.md`, `docs/help/faq-first-run.md`, `docs/providers/anthropic.md`, `docs/.i18n/zh-Hans-navigation.json`: every reference to `glm` / `providers/glm` updated to `providers/zai`.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Pure docs. No changes to runtime / `extensions/zai` / `src/plugins/provider-zai-endpoint.ts` / any plugin / CI / config schema. No standalone stub page kept at `/providers/glm` — the redirect in `docs.json` handles it.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

(Docs-only PR. UI / DX is checked because this is the first-touch onboarding doc for the Z.AI/GLM provider and directly shapes the new-user setup flow.)

## Linked Issue/PR

No prior issue — submitted as a direct contribution.

- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Motivation

The motivation comes from a real onboarding gap: many entry points (`docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/providers/index.md`, `docs/concepts/oauth.md`, `docs/help/faq-first-run.md`) surface `GLM models` first and route users to `/providers/glm`, which had its own self-contained setup. But the actual OpenClaw provider is `zai` (`ZAI_API_KEY`, Z.AI Chat Completions), so half of the operational information also lived under `/providers/zai`. Over time the two pages drifted, and "GLM is a model family hosted by Z.AI" — a key conceptual point — was hard to express cleanly across two separate pages. Consolidating to a single Z.AI page makes that relationship explicit at the top of the page and removes a recurring source of new-user confusion. The merge also lets us reflect the current onboarding behavior accurately: `openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-api-key` (and the regional `zai-coding-*` / `zai-cn` variants) already sets the default model, so the old "Set a default model" doc step is now stale and is removed in this PR.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Doc split between Z.AI and GLM produced two competing entry points and stale links. After the merge, every legacy URL (`/glm`, `/providers/glm`, `/zai`) must redirect to `/providers/zai`; the Z.AI page must render the full GLM model catalog and the merged Getting started; nav / providers index must not show a standalone GLM entry.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Mintlify dev preview. `npx --yes mintlify dev` on Linux, Node v24.15.0, started from `docs/` in a clean checkout of this branch. `docs/docs.json` uses Mintlify schema `https://mintlify.com/docs.json`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  cd docs
  npx --yes mintlify dev

  # In another terminal, once the preview is ready on localhost:3000:
  curl -sI http://localhost:3000/glm           | grep -iE 'HTTP|location'
  curl -sI http://localhost:3000/providers/glm | grep -iE 'HTTP|location'
  curl -sI http://localhost:3000/zai           | grep -iE 'HTTP|location'
  curl -sI http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -iE 'HTTP'
  curl -s   http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oiE '<title[^>]*>[^<]*</title>'
  curl -s   http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oiE 'glm-[0-9.a-z-]+|z\.ai|coding[- ]plan' | sort -u
  curl -s   http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oE 'Z_AI_API_KEY' | wc -l
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):**

  Terminal output from the local Mintlify preview (verbatim):

  ```
  $ curl -sI http://localhost:3000/glm | grep -iE 'HTTP|location'
  HTTP/1.1 307 Temporary Redirect
  location: /providers/zai

  $ curl -sI http://localhost:3000/providers/glm | grep -iE 'HTTP|location'
  HTTP/1.1 307 Temporary Redirect
  location: /providers/zai

  $ curl -sI http://localhost:3000/zai | grep -iE 'HTTP|location'
  HTTP/1.1 307 Temporary Redirect
  location: /providers/zai

  $ curl -sI http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -iE 'HTTP'
  HTTP/1.1 200 OK

  $ curl -s http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oiE '<title[^>]*>[^<]*</title>'
  <title>Z.AI - OpenClaw</title>

  $ curl -s http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oiE 'glm-[0-9.a-z-]+|z\.ai|coding[- ]plan' | sort -u
  Coding Plan
  GLM-5
  Z.AI
  glm-4.5
  glm-4.5-air
  glm-4.5-flash
  glm-4.5v
  glm-4.6
  glm-4.6v
  glm-4.7
  glm-4.7-flash
  glm-4.7-flashx
  glm-5
  glm-5-models
  glm-5-turbo
  glm-5.1
  glm-5v-turbo
  glm-models-naming-
  z.ai

  $ curl -s http://localhost:3000/providers/zai | grep -oE 'Z_AI_API_KEY' | wc -l
  6
  ```

  The 6 occurrences of `Z_AI_API_KEY` cover the auth row of the Property table, the "Auth details" accordion bullet, and the rendered React payload — confirming the legacy env var is documented in both prominent spots after the merge.

- **Observed result after fix:**
  - `/glm`, `/providers/glm`, and `/zai` all return `307 Temporary Redirect` with `location: /providers/zai`. (Production Mintlify may use a different redirect status, but the destination is the same and behavior is equivalent.)
  - `/providers/zai` returns `200 OK` with `<title>Z.AI - OpenClaw</title>`.
  - The rendered HTML contains the full GLM model list (`glm-4.5-air`, `glm-4.5-flash`, `glm-4.7-flash`, `glm-4.7-flashx`, `glm-5-turbo`, `glm-5-models`, `glm-models`) and `Z.AI` / `Coding Plan` sections — the original `glm.md` content has been merged in without loss.
  - Mintlify dev starts with no broken-link warnings; all previous links to `providers/glm` have been updated to `providers/zai`.
- **What was not tested:** Production Mintlify CDN cache. If any stale `/providers/glm` cache objects exist on the production deployment, they will either expire naturally or need to be purged on the deployment side — out of scope for this PR.
- **Before evidence (optional but encouraged):** The "before" state can be observed on the current production deployment: https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/glm and https://docs.openclaw.ai/providers/zai are still two separate pages with duplicated setup that has drifted apart.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Visitors to `/providers/glm` or `/glm` are redirected to `/providers/zai`. (`/glm` previously redirected to `/providers/glm`; the destination is updated. `/providers/glm` is a new redirect.)
- The docs site navigation and providers index no longer show a standalone `GLM models` entry. `Z.AI` is the single entry point and its subtitle mentions GLM.
- The Z.AI page now has a Property table and a Config example. Getting started is simplified from 3 steps (onboard → set default model → verify) to 2 steps (onboard → verify), reflecting that onboarding already sets the default model.
- The endpoint auto-detect description is corrected: the `zai-api-key` choice probes supported endpoints with the API key, rather than inferring from a key prefix.
- The legacy `Z_AI_API_KEY` env var (still accepted by the runtime; documented previously only on the deleted GLM page) is now documented on the consolidated Z.AI page — in the Property table auth row and the Auth details accordion. No behavior change for users who only set `ZAI_API_KEY`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

Docs-only — no runtime / auth / network behavior changes.

## Repro + Verification

See §Real behavior proof above — Environment, Steps, Expected, and Actual are all covered there.

- OS: Linux (Ubuntu)
- Runtime/container: Node v24.15.0; `npx --yes mintlify dev` (no global install)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): `docs/docs.json` (Mintlify schema)

## Evidence

- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets — see Real behavior proof / Evidence after fix
- [ ] Screenshot/recording — optional; terminal evidence is sufficient
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios:
  - All three legacy URLs (`/glm`, `/providers/glm`, `/zai`) correctly redirect to `/providers/zai` in the local Mintlify preview.
  - The rendered `/providers/zai` page contains the full GLM model list and the merged Getting started content.
  - Mintlify dev starts with no broken-link warnings.
  - Branch hygiene: ahead 1 commit relative to `origin/main`, diff limited to 10 files under `docs/`, no runtime / extensions / UI / superpowers files leaked into the PR.
- Edge cases checked:
  - Both `/glm` (existing redirect, destination updated) and `/providers/glm` (newly added redirect) paths are exercised.
  - The Chinese navigation `docs/.i18n/zh-Hans-navigation.json` also has `zh-CN/providers/glm` removed.
- What was **not** verified:
  - CDN cache purge on the production docs.openclaw.ai deployment (deployment-side concern).
  - Whether every GLM model id string in the page still corresponds to a callable model on Z.AI today — this PR only consolidates docs and does not change the bundled `zai` manifest.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes** — legacy URLs still resolve via redirects.
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: If the production Mintlify deployment has CDN-cached `/providers/glm` responses, users may see stale content briefly.
  - Mitigation: Cached entries will expire naturally, or a maintainer can purge them on the deployment side. The redirect itself is in `docs.json` and takes effect immediately once cache is cleared.
- Risk: External blog posts or old GitHub issues may still link to `/providers/glm`.
  - Mitigation: The `docs.json` redirect covers both `/providers/glm` and `/glm`, so external links continue to land users on the consolidated page. No external-link fix-up needed.
target.author: bobdu
